blurb: This is the place we end up at 2am after a night out or when my teenager wants pancakes at 7pm on a Tuesday. It's a classic neon-sign diner that's open 24/7, which is basically a miracle in the suburbs. The menu is massive and includes Greek stuff alongside all the usual diner fare. The breakfast is huge and good any time of day. My kids love the pancakes and I usually get the gyro platter because it's surprisingly decent. Perfect for late night cravings, post-game meals, or when you can't decide what you want because they literally have everything.
address: 306 Cobb Pkwy SE, Marietta, GA 30060, USA

blurb:
This place absolutely lives up to all the hype you've been hearing. The atmosphere is vibrant and fun, perfect for a night out with friends or when you want tacos that are way better than the usual suspects. The service is incredibly attentive, which is rare for casual Mexican places these days.

Start with the Esquite because it's packed with flavor and sets the tone for the whole meal. The Pineapple Guava pitcher and Passion Fruit pitcher are both refreshing and well-balanced - not too sweet or too strong. They know how to make drinks that actually complement the food instead of overwhelming it.

They're open until midnight most nights and 11pm on Sundays, so it works great for late dinners or when you get off work and want something good. With nearly 8,000 five-star reviews, there's obviously something special happening here.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb: This is our go-to for special occasions when we want to eat way too much meat. It's all-you-can-eat Brazilian barbecue where they bring different cuts right to your table. The cheese bread alone is worth the trip. My husband always gets excited about the picanha and the garlic steak. They also have feijoada stew which is hearty and perfect for cold nights. Great for birthdays, anniversaries, or when you want to impress out-of-town guests. Just come hungry because you'll definitely overdo it.
address: 1275 Powers Ferry Rd ste 230, Marietta, GA 30067, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Fri-Sat 10:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
This is where we go when we're craving real Indian food but don't want to deal with Buford Highway traffic. The dining room is colorful and comfortable, and they do family-style portions that work great for feeding a group or having leftovers for days. Perfect for when you want something different on a weeknight.

The biryani is what they're known for and it's worth the hype. The rice comes packed with tender meat and lots of flavor. Everything is served family-style so you can try a bunch of different dishes without ordering multiple entrees. They know how to handle spice levels too, so don't be afraid to ask for it mild if you're bringing kids.

Just know they break between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to get seated. Weekend hours are more flexible if you're doing a late lunch with the family.
address: 130 Perimeter Center Pl, Dunwoody, GA 30346, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:30 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 11:30 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb: This food hall is perfect when everyone in your group wants something different. It's got twenty different vendors so the kids can get pizza while you get sushi and your spouse gets barbecue. We usually end up here on weekends when we're walking around the square. The setup is casual and you can sit together even though you're eating completely different cuisines. Good for families with picky eaters or when you can't agree on one type of food. The variety is actually impressive for Marietta.
address: 68 North Marietta Pkwy NW, Marietta, GA 30060, USA
hours: Mon-Thu & Sun 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM, Fri-Sat 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
This place has completely changed my standards for pizza. The thin crust is perfectly crispy and they use ingredients that actually taste like something. It's not your typical pizza joint - more like what you'd get in Italy but right here in Sandy Springs. Great for date night or when you want pizza that feels a little special.

The traditional Italian style means thin crust and high-quality toppings, not the heavy cheese-loaded pies you might expect. They source ingredients from Italy and locally, and you can taste the difference. The salads and antipasti are also solid if you want to make it more of a full meal.

With a 4.9 rating from almost 5,000 reviews, there's usually a wait on weekends. But it moves pretty quickly and the pizza is worth planning ahead for.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
This is where you take someone when you want to impress them or celebrate something special. The setting right on the Chattahoochee is gorgeous, especially if you can snag a table by the windows. It's pricey but worth it for anniversaries, big birthdays, or when the in-laws are visiting and you want to show off a little.

The seafood is what they do best, and everything feels elevated without being fussy. The atmosphere is intimate and romantic, so it's perfect for date night or any time you want to feel fancy. Service is top-notch too, which you'd expect at this price point.

Sunday hours are a little weird with that break between brunch and dinner, so check before you head over. Parking can be tight on weekend evenings, so give yourself extra time.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 4:00 PM, 5:00 – 9:00 PM

blurb: We discovered this place when we wanted good Middle Eastern food without driving into town. It's in a strip mall but don't let that fool you. The shawarma is really good and the portions are generous. The falafel is crispy and not dry like some places. They have solid vegetarian options if you're feeding a mixed group. Good for a casual dinner when you want something different but not too fancy. The prices are really reasonable too, which is nice when you're feeding the whole family.
address: 1175 Franklin Gateway SE, Marietta, GA 30067, USA

blurb:
This is officially one of my top 5 dinner places in Atlanta, and I'm not exaggerating. The Persian food here is incredible and the whole experience feels special. Perfect for girls' night out or when you want to try something different that will actually blow you away. The atmosphere is sophisticated but still comfortable.

Start with the hummus and wings, then get the chicken shish kabobs with rice that has lentils and raisins. The combination of flavors is amazing and unlike anything else you'll find around here. The cocktails are also worth trying - they know what they're doing behind the bar. Save room for the tiramisu because it's the perfect way to end the meal.

They close for a few hours between lunch and dinner every day, so don't show up at 3pm expecting to get seated. But if you plan ahead, this place will become one of your go-to spots for special occasions.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb: This is the real deal French bakery that makes me feel fancy just walking in. The croissants are buttery and flaky like they should be, and the quiches are perfect for lunch. My wife loves their petit fours for special occasions. The baguettes are crusty on the outside and soft inside, just like in France. Great for weekend brunch or when you want to pick up something special for a dinner party. They close early on Sundays so don't wait too late if you're craving pastries after church.
address: 277 South Marietta Pkwy SW, Marietta, GA 30064, USA
hours: Mon-Sat 7:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Sun 9:00 AM – 3:30 PM
